transcript: Hello everyone. I'm Tarun, founder of CopperX. I started my journey as a freelancer from a small city in India. Oh, sorry. And... and later we built IconScout. A community of over 30... 10... 10,000 designers and millions of users worldwide. Our community was spread across emerging markets like India, Indonesia, Vietnam, Nigeria, and many more. The most important thing we learned is that talent is global, but financial inclusivity is not. When we spoke to community and freelancers across the globe, we realized that access to banking is a fundamental issue. Payments are often delayed for weeks, fees are insanely high, and people lose customers just because they can't receive payments. Imagine not being able to deliver a project just because your card keeps declining at AWS. And this is reality for millions of businesses across the globe. Let me share one of their stories. Freelancing is great, but there are days when I just sit idle without any projects, and sometimes I have so many projects that it's hard to meet all the deadlines. I love that freedom. I think getting paid can be frustrating sometimes. Not terrible. Just slower. Or more complicated than it needs to be. I've used other platforms before, but these platform charge high fees, and sometimes they just stop working. It breaks your momentum. Last month, a friend told me about CopperX, so I gave it a try. I got a U.S. account and started sharing it with my clients, and honestly, things just felt better. Payments came faster, and the fees were much lower. It remove a lot of usual stress. For me, freelancing isn't only about money. It's about choosing how I work, who I work with, and doing things I actually enjoy. It's freedom. And this is where the future is going. So, traditional finance is fragmented. And it was never meant to be built for the internet dollar. We are here to change that. Say hello to Kosh, a global bank for freelancers and small businesses. Doesn't matter where you are from and what you do. By using stablecoins and Solana, we are making receiving payments fast and globally accessible. Financial inclusion is a fundamental human right. And Kosh makes it easy to earn, save, and spend anywhere in the world. The same way WhatsApp removed borders for communication, we are removing borders for the money. Let me show you how Kosh works. Within five minutes, a freelancer or a small business gets a U.S. bank account and they can share the details with their global clients. And they start receiving money. Just like any business in the U.S. No delays, no hidden fees. Also, by using stablecoin powered cards, we can instantly enable them to pay for global tools which were blocked due to capital controls. And specially for all of you at Solana, we have built Pay Later mode. Simply deposit your SOL and keep spending. You decide when you want to pay back. Today or later. And many more. Sending privately, credit, and tons of other things. We built Kosh with the support of hundreds of users and backers and believers and our friends. I'm grateful to all of you for believing in us and I'm excited for the journey ahead. Thank you so much.

# Base–Solana Bridge Goes Live with Chainlink CCIP, Unlocking Massive Cross-Chain Liquidity
![Liam Turner](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%2048%2048%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)
By[Liam Turner](https://www.cryptoninjas.net/authors/liam/)
December 6, 2025
* 3min read
![base-solana](https://www.cryptoninjas.net/wp-content/uploads/base-solana.jpeg)
**Key Takeaways:**
* The Base–Solana Bridge is now live on mainnet, secured jointly by Chainlink CCIP and Coinbase.
* Users and developers can transfer SOL and SPL tokens to Base apps, enabling unified liquidity across both ecosystems.
* The launch marks one of the most significant cross-chain expansions for Base, strengthening its position as a hub for multichain Web3 activity.
The long-anticipated Base–Solana Bridge has officially[launched](https://x.com/chainlink/status/1996657107597160887?s=20), creating one of the most consequentia

tags.impact_reason: CopperX's Kosh offers a practical solution for freelancers using Solana stablecoins, addressing real-world payment issues, but doesn't represent a fundamental shift in the ecosystem.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a futuristic credit card as the Semantic Anchor because it's a universal symbol for financial access, directly addressing the freelancer's core problem of getting paid and making payments. The story is about a 'neobank' building new infrastructure on Solana, so I designed the card to be made of translucent, high-tech materials with glowing Solana circuitry inside, representing this fusion of traditional finance and DeFi. For the diegetic text, I integrated 'SOL' into the card's design where a number or logo might be. This is the collateral powering the system and anchors the visual to the specific technology mentioned in the headline, making it feel like a genuine artifact from this new financial world.
visual_idea.description: A sleek, futuristic credit card is presented vertically in a dramatic three-quarters view, floating against a dark, minimalist background. The card is crafted from frosted, semi-translucent acrylic, revealing intricate, glowing purple and green circuits pulsing softly within. The traditional chip is replaced by a geometric, luminous Solana logo. Where the cardholder's name might be, the ticker symbol 'SOL' is subtly laser-etched into the surface, catching the light as a core design element. The background is a deep, out-of-focus architectural space suggesting a modern data vault, with soft light sources creating a sense of depth and importance.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A sleek, futuristic credit card is presented vertically in a dramatic three-quarters view, floating against a dark, minimalist background. The card is crafted from frosted, semi-translucent acrylic, revealing intricate, glowing purple and green circuits pulsing softly within. The traditional chip is replaced by a geometric, luminous Solana logo. Where the cardholder's name might be, the ticker symbol 'SOL' is subtly laser-etched into the surface, catching the light as a core design element. The background is a deep, out-of-focus architectural space suggesting a modern data vault, with soft, volumetric light sources creating a profound sense of depth and importance. The entire scene is illuminated with sophisticated studio lighting, emphasizing the frosted acrylic texture and the vibrant glow of the internal circuitry, portraying the credit card as a premium neobank artifact. Composed for a vertical aspect ratio, the image emphasizes the card's monumental scale, with ample negative space at the top to highlight its epic presence. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, physically based rendering (PBR), cinematic lighting, volumetric light, dramatic chiaroscuro, depth of field, 3:4 aspect ratio.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a futuristic credit card as the Semantic Anchor because it's a universal symbol for financial access, directly addressing the freelancer's core problem of getting paid and making payments. The story is about a 'neobank' building new infrastructure on Solana, so I designed the card to be made of translucent, high-tech materials with glowing Solana circuitry inside, representing this fusion of traditional finance and DeFi. For the diegetic text, I integrated 'SOL' into the card's design where a number or logo might be. This is the collateral powering the system and anchors the visual to the specific technology mentioned in the headline, making it feel like a genuine artifact from this new financial world.
visual_idea.description: An extreme macro close-up on the corner of the futuristic credit card. The focus is on the luminous, geometric Solana logo that functions as the card's 'chip'. We see the polished edge of the frosted acrylic material and the intricate network of glowing purple and green circuits branching out just beneath the surface. The lighting is precise, casting a sharp highlight on the card's edge while the internal circuitry emits a soft, contained glow. The background is completely blurred into an abstract, dark gradient, emphasizing the card's high-tech materiality.

final_prompt.prompt_text: An extreme macro close-up, perfectly centered, on the sharp corner of a futuristic credit card, composed for a 1:1 square aspect ratio. The entire frame is filled with the high-tech materiality of the card. The central focus is an intricately luminous, geometric Solana logo, glowing brightly with an internal light, functioning as the card's 'chip'. We see the highly polished edge of the translucent frosted acrylic material, catching precise, sharp highlights from the volumetric studio lighting. Just beneath the surface, an intricate network of glowing purple and green circuits branches out, emitting a soft, contained glow that illuminates the frosted acrylic from within, showcasing the 'Glowing Circuitry' aesthetic. The overall aesthetic is that of a sleek 'Neobank Artifact'. The lighting is highly precise, casting a sharp, clean highlight along the card's polished edge, while the internal circuitry pulses with a contained, soft luminescence. The background is completely blurred into an abstract, dark gradient, emphasizing the card's cutting-edge design and high-tech materiality with zero distractions. Symmetrical composition, hyper-detailed textures, sharp focus, vibrant colors, cinematic, studio quality. 8k, octane render, raytracing, physically based rendering (PBR), hyper-detailed, intricate details.

editorial.headline: CopperX Debuts 'Kosh' Neobank with SOL-Collateralized Credit Lines
editorial.standfirst: CopperX has launched Kosh, a Solana-native neobank providing global freelancers with instant US accounts and credit lines backed by SOL holdings. The move signifies a shift from simple crypto payments to full-stack banking infrastructure on Solana.
key_takeaways: Launch of **Kosh**, a neobank offering US-domiciled bank accounts to global users in under 5 minutes.
Introduction of 'Pay Later' mode, allowing users to collateralize **SOL** for real-world spending without selling assets.
Strategic focus on the high-friction gig economy in emerging markets like India, Nigeria, and Vietnam.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
### The Shift to Full-Stack Neobanking

CopperX has moved beyond payment processing with the launch of **Kosh**, a dedicated neobank built on Solana. While the protocol layer of crypto has matured, the application layer often struggles to bridge the 'last mile' to traditional finance. Kosh addresses this by provisioning legitimate **US bank accounts** for freelancers and SMEs globally in under five minutes. This is not merely a wallet; it is a direct integration into the US banking system, powered by stablecoin rails. For institutional investors, this represents a critical evolution: Solana is no longer just a settlement layer but the backend infrastructure for a global challenger bank.

### Alpha: Collateralized Spending

The most significant 'Alpha' from the keynote is the introduction of the **Pay Later** mode. This feature allows users to deposit **SOL** as collateral to fund daily operations—such as paying AWS bills or vendor invoices—without liquidating their position. This mechanism effectively brings DeFi lending utility to a consumer-grade fintech app. For the user, it prevents taxable events and retains asset upside; for the ecosystem, it locks SOL supply while driving real-world velocity of money. This bridges the gap between holding crypto assets and needing fiat liquidity, a friction point that has historically hindered adoption among cash-flow-dependent businesses.

### Targeting the Global Gig Economy

Founder **Tarun Mangukiya** leverages his background with **IconScout** to target a specific, high-value demographic: the global freelance workforce in emerging markets. In regions like India, Nigeria, and Vietnam, access to US banking is a significant bottleneck. By positioning Kosh as the 'WhatsApp for money,' CopperX is bypassing local banking inefficiencies entirely. The ability to issue stablecoin-powered corporate cards that bypass local capital controls is a direct play on regulatory arbitrage, offering immediate utility to millions of underserved professionals. This is a clear case of product-market fit where blockchain is the invisible enabler, not the selling point.
